justifications.spatial_relations: Provides spatial relations that partially reflect the scene such as walking along the side but some relations are unverified or substitute for missing contextual labels.
justifications.overall: Good object coverage and extra semantic detail, but accuracy is reduced by a missing simple context for one person and questionable vehicle attribute assertions.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly identifies the pedestrian and several attributes but omits the parked vehicle present in the ground truth, reducing completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: The model captures basic pedestrian semantics like action and age but misses finer context details and any vehicle semantics, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations are provided even though the ground truth indicates the person is along the side of the road and a parked vehicle exists, so spatial information is largely absent.
justifications.overall: Reasonable identification of the pedestrian and key attributes but missing vehicle detection and spatial relations lead to a moderate overall score.

justifications.completeness: Very low completeness: the model reports only 3 pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains many more people (over 40) and one vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Low-to-moderate richness: some useful attributes are present (atomic action, simple context, age) but the extraction omits many per-person communicative and complex contextual labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Limited spatial reasoning: a few relations are provided but they are sparse, imprecise, and do not capture the dense set of spatial relations in the scene or any vehicle-relative relations.
justifications.overall: Overall poor performance driven by major detection and coverage failures despite some correct attribute assignments for the few detected pedestrians.

justifications.completeness: Very low coverage: the extraction lists only three pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth contains over thirty persons and two vehicles, missing the vast majority of entities and many context labels.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several per-object attributes (actions, age, vehicle states) which adds some richness, but it omits many entities and fails to capture communicative/contextual labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided coarse relative positions for the three detected pedestrians and a background vehicle which is somewhat informative, but it ignores spatial relations for most ground truth people and mischaracterizes side-of-road versus crossing contexts in several cases.
justifications.overall: Overall quality is low due to very poor completeness and only partial semantic and spatial information despite some correctly reported attributes.

justifications.completeness: Only three pedestrians and one vehicle were reported versus many annotated persons and the single vehicle has incorrect motion, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies attributes like age and atomic action but misses many communicative labels, object identifiers, and other ground truth labels, limiting semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some useful relative relations are provided (pedestrian-to-pedestrian and pedestrian-to-vehicle) but they are sparse and not validated against object ids or vehicle motion.
justifications.overall: While attribute-level descriptions are present, the extraction fails on object recall, has incorrect vehicle motion, and omits many ground truth semantics so overall quality is low.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians out of roughly 40 annotated and 1 of 2 vehicles, missing the vast majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes basic attributes (actions, age, vehicle state) but omits many fine-grained communicative/contextual labels and age variations present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides coarse left/center/right relations that are plausible for the few detected agents but omits spatial relations for most ground-truth objects and lacks precise localization.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some correct attribute assignments for a small subset of actors but major omissions in detection and fine-grained semantics lead to low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only two pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth contains many more pedestrians (including a child and seniors) and two vehicles, so it is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM includes multiple attribute fields (actions, context, age, trunk/doors) showing moderate richness but misses many per-person attributes present in the ground truth such as communicative behaviors and age diversity.
justifications.spatial_relations: The VLM provides left/right and in-crosswalk relations, but these appear inaccurate for most pedestrians who are annotated as walking along the side, so spatial relations are present but unreliable.
justifications.overall: Given the major omissions in detected entities and several incorrect spatial/contextual assignments, the overall quality is low.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only 3 pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists many more persons (around 28) and 2 vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction supplies multiple attribute fields (atomic_action, simple_context, age, etc.) showing some semantic structure but it mislabels ages (misses a child and a senior) and omits communicative tags present in ground truth, reducing its richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides only vague left/right relations among the three detected pedestrians and no relations to vehicles or the many other pedestrians, yielding weak and incomplete spatial information.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and partially incorrect, with low recall of entities and limited accurate semantics, resulting in poor practical utility for perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians and 1 vehicle out of 29 pedestrians and 2 vehicles in the ground truth, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes many attribute types per detected person (action, context, communicative, transporting, age) but misses many ground truth individuals and some age and communicative distinctions, so richness is moderate at best.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some plausible pairwise relations are provided but they are sparse and not comprehensively validated against the scene, resulting in limited spatial completeness and uncertain accuracy.
justifications.overall: Low detection completeness heavily lowers the overall score despite reasonable per-object attribute detail and a few spatial relations.

justifications.completeness: Identifies only three pedestrians and one vehicle while ground truth lists many more people and two vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Reports several attributes like actions, carrying status, and motion, but many attributes are incorrect or missing and some categories are misassigned.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides simple left/right and behind relations for a few entities but lacks comprehensive pairwise relations and does not align well with ground truth placements.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few correct atomic actions but misses most entities and contains multiple attribute errors, yielding poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Only three pedestrians were reported and vehicles were omitted, missing the majority of annotated people and both vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes several attribute fields but misses many communicative cues and mislabels ages, so it has limited semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relations are sparse and inconsistent (e.g., multiple entities labeled left of another) and do not cover most pedestrians.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a small subset of actors with some attributes but is largely incomplete and contains inaccuracies, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Very incomplete, the VLM detected only three pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth lists over thirty persons and two vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a variety of attribute types (clothing hints, actions, context, transporting, age) but misses many ground truth labels, mislabels context for many people, and lacks age diversity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some relative relations but they are vague and unanchored to object ids, cover only a few pedestrians, and the vehicle relation is coarse.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor due to major omissions and some incorrect contextual labels despite offering multiple attribute types.
